channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Michael Garron Hospital Adapts to Patient Surge, Converts Space for Care
item.description: Michael Garron Hospital in Toronto is facing an unprecedented surge in patients, far exceeding its original design capacity. Built to accommodate approximately 150 patients daily, the hospital now rou...

item.title: Gulf Desalination System Designed to Withstand Single-Point Disruptions
item.description: The Gulf region relies heavily on desalination for its freshwater supply, with countries like Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE producing the majority of their drinking water through this method. Desp...

item.title: Advance voting concludes in Terrebonne federal byelection amid potential impact on Liberal majority
item.description: On Monday, Terrebonne, Quebec saw the conclusion of advance voting in its federal byelection, which is taking place alongside two other byelections in Toronto. These elections are particularly signifi...

item.title: DJ Afro Explains Voice Change During Majembe vs Mbavu Destroyer Boxing Match
item.description: Kenya’s well-known movie commentator DJ Afro Amingos made a notable comeback at the recent Vurugu boxing event featuring Majembe and Mbavu Destroyer at Kasarani Indoor Arena. The fight went four round...

item.title: OpenAI Proposes Policy Measures to Mitigate AI-Driven Economic Disruption
item.description: OpenAI has outlined a set of policy proposals aimed at addressing the potential societal and economic disruptions caused by advanced artificial intelligence. The proposals include implementing taxes o...

item.title: Celebrating 50 Years of All The President’s Men: A Landmark in Investigative Journalism Cinema
item.description: All The President’s Men, directed by Alan J. Pakula and marking its 50th anniversary, remains an exceptional portrayal of investigative journalism. Based on the 1974 book by Bob Woodward and Carl Bern...

item.title: Edmunds Evaluates Geely Galaxy M9 SUV, Highlights Advanced Features and Competitiveness
item.description: US car-shopping platform Edmunds recently tested a Chinese-made SUV, the Geely Galaxy M9, marking its first evaluation of a vehicle from China. Despite Chinese cars being effectively banned in the Uni...

item.title: Egypt’s energy-saving curfew reshapes Cairo’s nightlife and economy
item.description: Egypt’s government has instituted new early-closing orders requiring most shops, restaurants, and malls in Cairo to shut by 9pm on weekdays and 10pm on weekends. The measure, in effect since April 3, ...

item.title: Supreme Court Orders CBI Inquiry into Alleged 1,200 Crore Contracts Favoured to Arunachal CM's Family
item.description: The Supreme Court has directed the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to investigate allegations of corruption in the allocation of public contracts worth over Rs 1,200 crore to companies associate...

item.title: Army Arrests Five Suspects After Teenage Girl Killed by Stray Bullet in Maiduguri
item.description: For Maiduguri, the Theatre Command of the Joint Task Force (North East) under Operation HADIN KAI don arrest five people wey dem suspect say dem use gun anyhow during one social event. The wahala begi...

item.title: Arsenal Players at Risk of Missing Second Leg Against Sporting Lisbon After Accumulated Yellow Cards
item.description: Arsenal are preparing to face Sporting Lisbon in the first leg of their UEFA Champions League quarter-final this Tuesday, hoping to turn around their recent poor run in domestic cup competitions. The ...

item.title: BOM issues severe warning for Tropical Cyclone Maila as it nears Far North Queensland
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology (BOM) has issued an updated warning for Tropical Cyclone Maila, upgrading its status to ‘severe’ as it moves towards the Australian coast. The cyclone, currently located in t...

item.title: Trump deems Iran peace proposal insufficient as Strait of Hormuz deadline nears
item.description: US President Donald Trump has described Iran’s recent peace proposal as a significant step but insufficient to meet US demands. Speaking during an Easter event at the White House, Trump warned that Ir...

item.title: Filipino Artists Showcase Hope Through Visual Storytelling in KWEN2RA Exhibition
item.description: ABS-CBN recently highlighted the 'KWEN2RA' exhibition, a vibrant showcase of Filipino artists using their talent to explore and address pressing social issues. This initiative, supported by ABS-CBN Fo...

item.title: Presidential Media Aide Onanuga Criticizes Peter Obi’s Political Moves Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: On Monday, April 6, 2026, Bayo Onanuga, who serves as Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Information and Strategy, fired fresh shots at Peter Obi, the former Labour Party presidential candida...

item.title: BC Ferries workers face challenges maintaining aging fleet amid delays and breakdowns
item.description: Employees of BC Ferries are working around the clock to maintain an aging fleet, as the company faces cancellations and delays due to mechanical issues and scheduled maintenance. The BC Ferry and Mari...

item.title: Mitsubishi to start producing hybrid cars in the Philippines
item.description: Mitsubishi has announced its plans to begin manufacturing hybrid cars in the Philippines, a move that could potentially boost the local automotive industry and promote sustainable mobility in the coun...

item.title: Nigeria Leads Africa in Food Waste, EU Raises Concern Over Environmental and Economic Impacts
item.description: Nigeria is currently the largest contributor to food waste in Africa, with an estimated 38 million tonnes of food wasted annually, a situation the European Union (EU) has described as alarming. The EU...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV's First Easter Blessing Calls for Peace in the Middle East Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: In his first Easter blessing as pontiff, Pope Leo XIV called on world leaders, particularly those with the power to wage war, to choose peace as the Middle East remains embroiled in conflict. The war,...

item.title: Home Office launches programme to support schools in high-risk areas against knife crime
item.description: The Home Office in England has announced a £1.2 million initiative to provide targeted support to schools at greatest risk of knife crime. The programme, part of a broader government pledge to halve k...
